title: Plakar PostgreSQL AWS IAM Exporter Connector Config

location.description: PostgreSQL connection URI: postgres+aws://[user@]host[:port][/database]. No password component — the IAM authentication token is fetched automatically from AWS.

host.description: RDS instance hostname (default: localhost). Overrides the host in the location URI.

port.description: RDS instance port (default: 5432). Overrides the port in the location URI.

username.description: PostgreSQL username to authenticate as (required). Must be an IAM-enabled database user. Overrides the username in the location URI.

region.description: AWS region of the RDS instance (required), e.g. eu-west-3.

database.description: Target database for pg_restore when restoring a single-database dump. If omitted, the database name is inferred from the dump filename.

create_db.description: When true, passes -C to pg_restore so the target database is created from the archive metadata.

restore_globals.description: When true, feeds globals.sql to psql before restoring the database dump.

no_owner.description: When true, passes --no-owner to pg_restore so that ALTER OWNER commands are skipped.

exit_on_error.description: When true, passes -e to pg_restore and ON_ERROR_STOP=1 to psql so restore stops on the first error.

schema_only.description: When true, passes -s to pg_restore to restore only schema (no data). Mutually exclusive with data_only.

data_only.description: When true, passes -a to pg_restore to restore only data (no schema). Mutually exclusive with schema_only.

pg_bin_dir.description: Directory containing the PostgreSQL client binaries (pg_restore, psql). When omitted, binaries are resolved via $PATH.

ssl_mode.description: SSL mode for the connection, passed via PGSSLMODE. IAM authentication requires an encrypted connection — use `require` or higher.

ssl_cert.description: Path to the client SSL certificate file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLCERT.

ssl_key.description: Path to the client SSL private key file (PEM format), passed via PGSSLKEY.

ssl_root_cert.description: Path to the root CA certificate used to verify the server (PEM format), passed via PGSSLROOTCERT.
